name: Double-Leg Heel Raises
description: Stand with hands on a stable surface, feet shoulder-width apart, equal weight on both legs. Lift heels over a 2-second count, hold briefly, then lower slowly over 4 seconds.

name: Seated Passive-Assisted Knee Extensions
description: Seated in a chair, place the foot of your non-operative leg under the heel of your surgical leg. Gently use your good leg to lift your surgical leg out as straight as possible while keeping it relaxed. Return to start.

name: Straight Leg Raise
description: Lie on your back, bend your non-surgical leg. Keep the surgical leg straight and raise it to the height of your bent knee. Hold briefly, then lower slowly.

name: Single-Leg Heel Raises
description: Stand with hands on stable surface, feet shoulder-width apart. Rise onto both toes over 2 seconds, then bend the non-operative knee to stand only on the operative foot. Lower slowly over 4 seconds.

name: Standing Hip Flexion With Resistance
description: Loop a resistance band around a secure object. Place one ankle inside the loop and step back until there is slight tension. Stand tall, tighten the quad to straighten the knee. Lift the banded leg slightly and kick forward slowly. Return slowly.

name: Prone Hamstring Curls
description: Lie face down with legs straight. Curl your surgical leg toward your glutes, hold briefly, then lower slowly. Strengthens the hamstrings before surgery.

name: Heel Slides
description: Slide your heel toward your buttocks, bending the knee as far as comfortable. Slide back out.

name: Standing Single-Leg Hip Extension With Resistance
description: Band looped around a secure object, one ankle inside, step back for tension. Stand tall, tighten the quad to lock the knee. Lift the leg in the band slightly and kick it backward slowly. Return slowly.

name: Running Progression
description: Progressive running from walking on treadmill → jogging → running → straight-line jog-runs on even surfaces (e.g., track). Increase speed and duration as tolerated with proper landing mechanics.

name: Ankle Pumps
description: Move your foot up and down at the ankle, like pressing a gas pedal. Keep it rhythmic.

name: Standing Single-Leg Hip Abduction With Resistance
description: Band looped to a secure anchor at ankle height, step sideways for slight tension. Stand tall. Lift the banded leg slightly and move it out to the side, keeping the knee straight. Avoid leaning the trunk. Return slowly.

name: Double-Leg Quarter Squats
description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hands on waist. Bend slightly at hips, then at knees, lowering buttocks about 6 inches. Keep weight evenly on both legs, trunk straight, and knees aligned over 2nd/3rd toes.

name: Prone Hangs
description: Lie face down with your knee and lower leg hanging off the edge of the bed. Let gravity straighten the knee.

name: Cutting and Deceleration
description: Higher-level cutting, deceleration, and reactive movements: 45-degree cuts, wide S-curves, figure-eight running, start-stop exercises. Performed under supervision with focus on proper landing mechanics and no valgus collapse.

name: Single-Leg Balance
description: Stand near a wall or chair for safety. Balance on your surgical leg for up to 30 seconds. Focus on a fixed point ahead. Trains proprioception before surgery.

name: Seated Knee Extension
description: Sit on a chair, slowly straighten your knee as far as possible. Hold at the top, lower slowly.

name: Single-Leg Plyometrics
description: Single-leg hopping with functional movements: hopping in place, hopping forward/lateral, sport-specific plyometric patterns. Progress only when double-leg form is consistently solid and strength testing criteria are met.

name: Standing Hamstring Curls
description: Stand holding a chair for balance. Bend your knee, bringing your heel toward your buttocks.

name: Standing Anterior Reach
description: Stand straight, hands at lower abdomen. Bend one knee as you reach the opposite foot forward as far as possible under control. Lightly touch the heel down for stability. Return to standing.

name: Standing Hip Hinge
description: Balance on one leg with knee slightly bent. Keep the other leg straight. Hinge forward at the hips, torso toward the ground until a slight hamstring stretch is felt, while the straight leg lifts behind you. Keep the low back neutral. Return slowly.

name: Wall Slides
description: Lean your back against a wall. Slide down into a shallow squat (30-45 degrees). Hold.

name: Everyday Knee Extensions
description: When resting, sitting or lying down, extend your surgical leg in front of you with the knee straight up. Allow gravity to gently continue to straighten the knee. Also perform supine or prone knee extensions before and after heel slides.

name: Go for a Walk
description: A short walk to promote circulation, reduce stiffness, and improve gait. Start with 5–10 minutes and increase duration as tolerated. Walk on flat ground, use crutches if needed.

name: Side Step Up
description: Stand beside a 2–6 inch step or platform. Place the nearest foot on the step. Lift yourself up through the heel while simultaneously lifting the opposite knee to 90°. Return to start slowly, stepping completely off the step.

name: Patellar Mobilization
description: Gently push your kneecap up, down, left, and right with your thumbs. Move slowly.

name: Front Step Ups
description: Stand behind a 6-inch step. Place one foot on the step and step up, weight through the heel. As you step up, lift the other knee forward until the thigh reaches 90° at the hip. Return slowly, stepping completely off the step. Switch legs.

name: Supine Knee Extensions
description: Lie on your back with your leg straight. Place a small towel roll under your heel — not your knee — and hold that position.

name: Basic Plyometrics — Double-Leg
description: Double-leg hopping in place with proper mechanics and no pain. Landing focus: knees vertically aligned with toes, equal weight distribution, no inward knee bend (valgus collapse). Progress to single-leg hopping only when double-leg form is solid.

name: Short Arc Quads
description: Place a rolled towel under your knee. Straighten just the lower leg, lifting your foot. Hold, lower.

name: Prone Knee Extensions
description: Lie on your stomach with a small flat towel roll just above your knee on the thigh. Let your foot hang off the end of the bed or table.

name: Hamstring Sets
description: Sit with knee slightly bent. Press your heel into the floor without moving. Hold the contraction.

name: Gastrocnemius Stretch
description: Stand facing a wall. Step the surgical leg back, keep it straight, and press the heel down. Lean into the wall.

name: Hamstring Set — on Your Back
description: Lie on your back and bend your knee to bring your calf to 90 degrees. Push your heel down into the surface with mild to moderate pressure. Hold 6 seconds, then relax.

name: Terminal Knee Extensions (TKE)
description: Loop a band behind your knee, anchored in front. Start slightly bent, then straighten against the resistance.

name: Hamstring Set — on Your Stomach
description: Lie on your stomach with a towel under the front lower part of your thigh. Bend your knee to bring your calf up to 90 degrees from the surface.

name: Quadriceps Set
description: Lie on your back or sit with legs out straight. Tighten the top of your thigh, pushing your knee flat against the surface so the kneecap moves toward your hip. Focus purely on the quad — not glutes or hamstrings. Hold 5 seconds, then relax.

name: Hamstring Set — Standing
description: Stand on both feet with both crutches for support. Perform a gentle hamstring curl, bending your knee upward behind you. Hold 6 seconds, then relax.

name: Standing Hip Extensions
description: Stand tall with hands on a stable surface. Shift weight to the non-operative leg. With an upright trunk, lift the operative leg slightly behind you without rotating pelvis or trunk. Hold 2 seconds, return slowly.

name: Hamstring / Quadriceps Co-Contraction
description: Lie on your back with the operative leg straight. Push your heel down into the surface as if digging in, then simultaneously tighten your quadriceps to try to straighten the leg. Push firmly but not enough to lift your body. Hold both sides of the thigh contracted for 5 seconds, then relax.

name: Side-Lying Hip Abduction
description: Lie on your non-operative side. Straighten the top (operative) leg, bend the bottom leg for comfort. Bring the top leg slightly behind your trunk with toes rotated in slightly. Lift the top leg using the hip — not back or side. Lower and repeat.

name: Prone Straight Leg Raises
description: Lie on your stomach, legs shoulder-width apart. Tighten core and glutes. Lift only the operative leg off the surface, hold 2 seconds, then lower slowly. Do not rotate hips or arch your back.

name: Straight Leg Raises With Resistance
description: Same as Straight Leg Raises but with added resistance (weight or band). Lie on back, non-operative leg bent to 90°. Tighten the operative quad fully, lift to the height of the other knee using a slow and controlled motion. Return slowly.

name: Side-Lying Hip Adduction
description: Lie on your side with operative leg down and extended straight. Pull the non-operative foot toward you and place it in front of your operative knee. Keep trunk and hips stable. Lift the operative leg off the surface, hold 2 seconds, lower slowly.

name: Standing Terminal Knee Extension
description: Stand with a resistance band around the back of the operative knee, attached to a stable anchor. Begin with knee slightly bent, heel and foot on the floor, trunk upright. Straighten the knee using the quad muscle. Hold 6 seconds. Return slowly.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
description: Stand facing away from a bench, rear foot elevated. Lower your front knee toward the ground, keeping your front shin vertical. This is the gold-standard single-leg strength exercise for ACL rehab.

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
description: Kneel on a mat with feet anchored. Slowly lower your torso toward the ground using only your hamstrings. Catch yourself with your hands at the bottom. One of the best hamstring strengtheners in the research.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
description: Stand on your surgical leg. Hinge forward at the hip while raising your non-surgical leg behind you. Keep your back flat. Trains hip strength and balance simultaneously.

name: Heavy Leg Press (Single Leg)
description: On a leg press machine, use your surgical leg only. Load should progress toward 1.25× bodyweight. Essential for building the quad strength needed for plyometrics.

name: Step-Down Exercise
description: Stand on a step on your surgical leg. Lower your non-surgical foot toward the ground slowly, then return. Excellent for quad control and detecting valgus collapse.

name: Lateral Band Walk
description: Place a resistance band around your ankles. Walk sideways for 10 steps each direction with knees slightly bent. Targets hip abductors and improves frontal plane stability.
